title: Escape From the Chateau
description: On pages 12–15, this part focuses on Unknown person #9, Unknown person #10, Unknown person #11, Sanji, Capone Bege, Big Mom Pirates as The tea party collapses into chaos as the alliance tries to flee Big Mom’s territory.
scene: The assassination plan fails and the wedding becomes a desperate escape from a collapsing battlefield.

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: That was the head chef's doing, huh...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I thought that was curtains for us, to be honest.

dialogue.speaker: Streusen
dialogue.text: Peh! Sweets made with Streusen's transformation powers

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Fill the belly, but they taste terrible.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: Ministers! What in the world just happened?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I thought the city would be crushed, and that'd be that...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They got away, didn't they?! They should still be somewhere around here!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Send word to every last island tart in Totland!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Don't let a single member of either the straw hat pirates or the firetank pirates escape!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: NorthWest part of whole cake island

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Thank you, Bege! I really thought we were done for, but...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Quit it with the thanks. Makes me feel like I did something good. Gross.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I'm the one that carried you lot out here, though!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Shut up, trash! Do you remember what you were doing to those kids on punk hazard?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We've no use for you anymore... just go die or something.

summary: This section advances the Whole Cake Island Escape storyline with 37 dialogue beats. The alliance survives the failed tea party by turning the chaos into a retreat.
